rulings.Case Title: DAVID ROBERTS VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 21, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Mack Trucks, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CARROLL CLINE et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Boss Manufacturing Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 21, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Boss Manufacturing Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to causes of action 1, 2 and 4 (negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium) and off calendar as to punitive damages. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Cline¿s deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Ay¿s declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Cline was exposed to asbestos-containing gloves attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs have dismissed their claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
